creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
(In reply to Brian from comment #7)

I only have access to 4 GiByte and 8 GiByte RPi4B's. But there is a
way to have config.txt set up for using a smaller subset of the RAM.
So at some point I might test if doing so shows a problem in my
context.

I use a serial console configuration and the program that handles the
serial connection can record the bytes into a file that it generates.
I extracted the text from such a log file that recorded from power-on
to the login prompt (at which point I stopped the logging).

I do boot RaspiOS64 (my abbreviation) on each RPi4B to deal with
EEPROM updates.
